info.name: InvoSync API — Customers, Invoices & Automations
info.description:
Curated Postman collection for InvoSync's `/api/v1` — scoped to the routes used by the n8n Customer Onboarding and Invoice Creation automations, for building n8n custom nodes around them.

## Setup
1. Duplicate the collection into your workspace.
2. Open **Variables** (collection-level) and fill in:
   - `base_url` — InvoSync API base: `http://localhost:3000/api/v1` in dev, `https://invosync.co.ke/api/v1` in production.
   - `n8n_base_url` — your n8n instance base: `http://localhost:5678` in dev, `https://n8n.zyncapi.com` in production. Used to build the example webhook URLs below.
   - `api_key` — a platform-admin InvoSync API key (`isk_admin_...`), generated from Admin → Settings → API Keys (app/admin/settings/api-keys). n8n workflows run cross-tenant (one registered automation can fire for any workspace), so they authenticate with a single admin key rather than a separate tenant key per workspace. Grant it the `admin:tenant-data` scope — that alone is sufficient to exercise any tenant scope (`customers:read`, `customers:write`, `invoices:read`, `invoices:write`, ...) for whichever workspace is named in `X-Workspace-Id`, per lib/api-context.ts requireScope().
   - `workspace_id` — the workspace to act on for this request. InvoSync automation webhook payloads always include `customer.id`'s owning workspace as `workspaceId` in `lib/automations.ts` triggerAutomation() — an n8n workflow should set this per-execution from that value rather than hardcoding it.
   - `customer_id`, `invoice_id` — fill in from earlier responses as you go.
3. Every request below sends two headers for security: `Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}` (collection-level, applies automatically) and `X-Workspace-Id: {{workspace_id}}` (per-request — required by admin keys to scope which tenant's data is being read/written; see lib/api-context.ts resolveWorkspaceId()). If you use a tenant key (`isk_test_`/`isk_live_`) instead, the workspace is already bound to the key and `X-Workspace-Id` is simply ignored.
4. In n8n, the same `base_url`/`api_key` pair becomes a "Header Auth" credential (`Authorization: Bearer <api_key>`) attached to HTTP Request nodes that call back into InvoSync, with `X-Workspace-Id` set per-execution from the webhook payload's `customer`/`invoice` workspace — see notes/n8n-workflows/*.json.

## Full API surface
This collection is a curated subset. The full OpenAPI 3.1 spec (every `/api/v1` route) is always available at `{{base_url}}/openapi.json` and can be imported wholesale into Postman or n8n.

## Webhook payloads (InvoSync → n8n)
The "Automation Webhook Payloads" folder below isn't live requests — it's saved example bodies of what InvoSync POSTs to a registered n8n webhook for `customer.created` and `invoice.created`, for reference when defining a custom node's expected input. Their URLs use `{{n8n_base_url}}` so the same collection works against your local n8n and n8n.zyncapi.com without editing each request.

item.name: Customers
item.description: Backed by services/customers.ts via lib/api-context.ts requireWorkspace — same logic path as the dashboard.

header.description: Which workspace's customers to read — required for admin keys, ignored for tenant keys.

request.description: Requires scope customers:write (or admin:tenant-data + X-Workspace-Id). Triggers the customer.created automation (if one is registered in Admin → Automations) and the customer welcome email.

item.name: Invoices
item.description: Backed by services/invoices.ts. Creating a type=INVOICE document triggers the invoice.created automation (if registered).

header.description: Which workspace's invoices to read — required for admin keys, ignored for tenant keys.

request.description: Emails the branded invoice document to the customer (lib/workspace-email.ts).

item.name: Automation Webhook Payloads (reference only)
item.description: Not live requests. Saved example bodies of what InvoSync POSTs to a registered n8n webhook, for defining an n8n custom node's expected input. See lib/automations.ts.

request.description: `workspaceId` is what an n8n HTTP Request node reads to set `X-Workspace-Id` on any callback into InvoSync's API (see the InvoSync API credential note above) — it's the workspace the customer/invoice belongs to, always included by lib/automations.ts triggerAutomation(). The `integrations` block only appears when the automation has Linked Integrations configured (QuickBooks/Zoho CRM here) and that workspace has an approved+active connection with saved credentials — see the "Linked Integrations" section of AdminAutomationsClient. `{{n8n_base_url}}` resolves to your local instance in dev and n8n.zyncapi.com in production without editing this request.

total is in cents (KES 5,800.00 = 580000), matching Invoice.total's storage convention. `workspaceId` sets X-Workspace-Id on any InvoSync API callback; `{{n8n_base_url}}` resolves per environment without editing this request.
